item.title: Work with Codex from anywhere
item.description: Use Codex anywhere with the ChatGPT mobile app. Monitor, steer, and approve coding tasks in real time across devices and remote environments.

item.title: Browsers Treat Big Sites Differently
item.description: Safari and Firefox change how big sites render based on the domain. TikTok, Netflix, Instagram… even SeatGuru. Chrome doesn’t. Why is that?
item.author: (Den Odell)

item.title: GitHub - DepthFirstDisclosures/Nginx-Rift: exploit for CVE-2026-42945
item.description: exploit for CVE-2026-42945. Contribute to DepthFirstDisclosures/Nginx-Rift development by creating an account on GitHub.

item.title: Req Is All You Need
item.description: Req and ServerSentEvents cover all your Elixir AI needs.

item.title: How Poor Sleep Drove Me Insane, and My Long Path to Recovery
item.description: I'm sharing my experience dealing with what might be the most underdiagnosed and under-treated health disorder today.

item.title: minimal structures
item.description: I build organic minimal structures.

item.title: Four tech waves. Six companies. Here’s what I’m building next.
item.description: I’ve been lucky. Lucky to be building during four major technology shifts: the internet, SaaS/web services, mobile/social, and now AI, and to have learned something useful from each one. Not by being the smartest person in the room, but by being around others building at the same

item.title: Working Hard | Joy of Reading
item.description: Before podcasts were a thing, Bill Simmons would trade e-mails with somebody successful for his piece on ESPN called "The Curious Guy". I ran into this excha...

item.title: I owe my career to open-source communities. I'm not sure newcomers can say the same.
item.description: Open-source communities shaped my career in ways no tutorial ever could. Now, as AI floods those same spaces with noise, I worry about the people arriving just a few years too late.

item.title: Agents Can Reason. They Still Can't Really Search.
item.description: Agents have a search problem across the whole stack: web search, RAG, tool discovery, skills/workflow loading, and even context compaction.
item.author: (Dipkumar Patel)

item.title: May I recommend… understanding Emacs's patterns
item.description: The above, unless I've completely forgotten my Shakespeare, was a soliloquy from Juliet Consulet regarding Romeo Montagnu

item.title: The End of Claude Code Automation
item.description: Starting June 15, Anthropic is moving automated Claude Code usage out of the normal subscription bucket. usage will now count against a separate monthly API-style credit that is much smaller than what…

item.title: Stop using user passwords for OpenStack automation
Simon Rodriguez
item.description: Renderistically - Simon Rodriguez

item.title: Noam Hurwitz
item.description: Modern chips are produced in part through lithography: light is projected through a mask onto photosensitive material coating silicon to pattern transistors, contacts, vias, interconnects, and other structures. As Moore’s Law pushes on and chips continue to pack more computational capacity into smaller surface areas, lithography machines need increasingly fine precision.

item.title: The Internet is not dead
what's the ideal analogy
item.description: Alok Singh's Blog

item.title: Naturally Occurring Quasicrystals
item.description: I love quasicrystals—like crystals, but with patterns that never repeat, like Penrose tiles. But they’ve very rare in nature. They’re created only by the most exotic and violent e…

item.title: Small Model Forensics
item.description: What ~2,000 timed API calls reveal about how closed-weight model providers scale inference, or fail to, in 2026.

item.title: Cheap agents, alumni shirts, and Elias Thorne · Daniel May
item.description: On agent-coded cold outreach, the alumni t-shirt scam, the Elias Thorne convergence, and what gets visible when no one does the work.

item.title: Don't Be Discouraged to Code by Hand
item.description: Am allowed to learn Lua now?

item.title: God Damn AI is making me dumb
item.description:
It's so god damn tempting to use AI to write. Whether it is articles, code, or documents. I feel like using AI is diminishing my ability to write myself.
...

item.title: Bitcoin trader recovers $400,000 using Claude AI after getting 'stoned' and losing wallet password 11 years ago — bot tried 3.5 trillion passwords before decrypting an old wallet backup
item.description: The user apparently changed the password while 'stoned.'

item.title: Introducing Claude for Small Business
item.description: We're launching Claude for Small Business, a package of connectors and ready-to-run workflows that put Claude inside the tools small businesses use every day.
